amended_continuation_decision.do_not: Do not import, patch, or promote FL17 in this stage. Do not treat FL17 as baseline. Do not continue without visible SEE/CE evidence.
amended_continuation_decision.next_correct_command: Run exactly one bounded FL17-MERGE-1 staging and diff classification stage with SEE/CE preflight: load GOV_REPAIR_SEE_CE_0_REPORT plus FL17-COMPARE-0 and FL17-MERGE-PLAN-0 artifacts, run current web.run research/improvement check appropriate to merge governance if needed, process SEE/CE, extract/probe target tracker-working OS and FL17 candidate to staging, compute hash-based file deltas, classify import/retain/exclude/conflict groups, write delta report, receipt, and handoff, and stop before importing or patching.

boot.note: The live extracted canonical root was not present in /mnt/data during this stage, so the stage used uploaded ZIPs, checksums, prior receipt directories, and PROMPT_ENGINE_v2.md as authority evidence without mutating OS contents.

constraints: All subsequent FL17 merge stages must explicitly include a SEE/CE preflight or a written applicability exception before execution.

Prior FL17-COMPARE-0 and FL17-MERGE-PLAN-0 artifacts are governance-incomplete because their visible receipts do not show SEE/CE preflight, but their core local artifact findings remain materially valid.
The next stage may proceed only if it loads this GOV repair report and treats it as an amendment to those prior stage receipts.
Do not promote or patch FL17 until tracker lifecycle enforcement, FL16/FL17 semantic split, and baseline authority conflict are validated with evidence.
Tracker status must remain compact, render-first, evidence-bound, and mobile-safe.
ce.rationale: The prior compare/merge-plan conclusions are supported by direct archive/receipt evidence and are consistent with current governance research. The defect is missing governance preflight evidence, not necessarily wrong classification. This report repairs the process lineage by explicitly binding SEE/CE constraints before the next import/patch stage.

prior_stage_audit.amendment: Bind this GOV_REPAIR_SEE_CE_0_REPORT as a required authority for any next FL17 merge/import stage.
prior_stage_audit.reason: Both stages performed useful artifact comparisons/planning and did not patch/promote. However, their receipts do not demonstrate the required current research + SEE + CE preflight demanded for governed MetaBlooms work.

scope: governance repair audit only; no FL17 import, patch, or promotion

limitations: This stage did not patch or import FL17 artifacts.
Current canonical extracted root /mnt/data/Metablooms_OS was not present; ZIP/receipt artifacts were used as authority evidence.
see.research_question: Does the FL17 compare/merge-plan workflow remain valid after correcting the skipped SEE/CE governance preflight?

source_index.claim: AI governance should manage risks through deliberate design/development/use/evaluation practices and maintain trustworthiness considerations.
ChatGPT UI/tool/project capabilities change over time, so platform-dependent workflow planning requires current source checks.
Progress, success, waiting, and error information should be explicit status information rather than hidden context changes.
Architecture descriptions should include views governed by viewpoints, correspondence/consistency rules, known issues, and evaluation methods.

validated_claims.claim: MetaBlooms governance should not be optional or selectively applied; governance gates should be mapped, measured, managed, and recorded as part of risk management.
Because ChatGPT UI/tool/project behavior changes, MetaBlooms stages that depend on ChatGPT behavior should include a current-source preflight or explicit bounded exception.
Inline tracker status changes are a status-message problem and should remain explicit, visible, and evidence-bound.
The OS architecture and merge plan should document views/concerns, known issues, consistency rules, and evaluation methods rather than relying on informal judgement.
